mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant at p > 0.10 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive slope flips positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% force aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 3.5% is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal consumption expenditures accelerates signals unsustainable consumer borrowing, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant at p > 0.10 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An upward inflection in the industrial production index during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid supply chain normalization and earnings rebound, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO inflections during post-shock recoveries are followed by negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the predictive relationship vanishes with Rank IC < 0.02.
